title: A.I. Start-Ups From Canada and Germany Merge to Take On Silicon Valley
description: Cohere is acquiring Aleph Alpha in a deal aimed at customers uneasy about the dominance of American companies in artificial intelligence.
content:
Struggling to find a foothold in the increasingly competitive market for artificial intelligence, two start-ups on Friday said they were merging in a trans-Atlantic alliance to better compete with American tech giants.

title: Spain's job-hungry youngsters turn to vocational training
description: With almost a quarter of under-25s in Spain out of work, many are now turning their backs on traditional university degrees and opting instead for technical training that can withstand the threat of artificial intelligence on white collar work.
content: According to data from the report "Education at a Glance 2025. OECD Indicators", 39 percent of students aged 15 to 19 in Spain are opting for vocational training courses instead of university. This rate has increased by nine percent over the past six... [2665 chars]

content: New Delhi, April 24 India’s 'techade' will be shaped by emerging technologies such as 6G, Artificial Intelligence (AI) and quantum computing, as the country builds on its strong telecom transformation, Dr Pemmasani Chandra Sekhar, Minister of State f... [3250 chars]

title: Over 16,000 Claude users worry AI will take their jobs, finds Anthropic
description: A large-scale study of Claude users reveals a growing unease around artificial intelligence in the workplace. While many report significant productivity gains, a sizeable share worry that the same tools boosting efficiency today could ultimately make their roles redundant, especially among early-career professionals and highly exposed occupations.
content:
The promise of artificial intelligence has always come with a quiet, persistent question: what happens to the humans?
A new study by Anthropic, based on responses from 81,000 users of its Claude AI system, suggests that this question is no longer the... [3561 chars]

title: Trump administration vows crackdown on Chinese companies 'exploiting' AI models made in US
description: The Trump administration is vowing to crack down on foreign tech companies’ exploitation of U.S. artificial intelligence models, singling out China at a time that country is narrowing the gap with the U.S. in the AI race. In a Thursday memo, Michael Kratsios, the president’s chief science and technology adviser, accused foreign entities “principally based in China” of engaging in deliberate, industrial-scale campaigns to “distill,” or extract capabilities from, leading AI systems made in the U.S. and “exploiting American expertise and innovation.” China’s embassy in Washington said it opposed “the unjustified suppression of Chinese companies by the U.S.”
content:
WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration is vowing to crack down on foreign tech companies' exploitation of U.S. artificial intelligence models, singling out China at a time that country is narrowing the gap with the U.S. in the AI race.

title: Microsoft rolls out buyouts for 7% of US employees as AI investments grow
description: The software giant is offering voluntary buyouts to a section of its US workforce under a ‘rule of 70’ programme, as it shifts focus to artificial intelligence and cloud.
content: The software giant said that the move is intended to provide greater flexibility for employees who may be considering retirement or a transition out of the company. Moreover, it also aligns with broader internal changes as the tech giant reallocates ... [1219 chars]
